volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 4.2.695 - Section 4.4.745)

sections.label: Section 4.2.695
sections.original: Non si capiundos mihi sciam esse inimicos omnes homines .
sections.translation: not if I were to know that all men would be my enemies in consequence . Her have I chosen for mine ; she has fallen to my lot ; our feelings are congenial ; farewell they , who wish for a separation between us ; nothing but Death separates her from me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.698
sections.translation: I begin to revive .

sections.label: Section 4.2.698b
sections.original: Non Apollinis magis verum atque hoc responsum est .
sections.translation: Not the responses of Apollo are more true than this . If it can possibly be contrived that my father may not believe

sections.label: Section 4.2.700
sections.original: Quo minus hae fierent nuptiae , volo . Sed si id non poterit ,
sections.translation: that this marriage has been broken off through me , I could wish it . But if that can not be , I will do that which is easily effected , for him to believe that through me it has been caused . What do you think of me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.702b
sections.translation: That you are as unhappy as myself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.702c
sections.translation: I ’ m contriving an expedient .

sections.label: Section 4.2.702d
sections.translation: You are a clever hand ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.703
sections.translation: if you do set about any thing .

sections.label: Section 4.2.703b
sections.original: Hoc ego tibi profecto effectum reddam .
sections.translation: Assuredly , I ’ ll manage this for you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.704
sections.translation: There ’ s need of it now .

sections.label: Section 4.2.704b
sections.translation: But I ’ ve got it now .

sections.label: Section 4.2.704c
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.704d
sections.original: Huic , non tibi habeo , ne erres .
sections.translation: For him ( pointing to PAMPHILUS ) I ’ ve got it , not for you , don ’ t mistake .

sections.label: Section 4.2.705
sections.translation: I ’ m quite satisfied .

sections.label: Section 4.2.705b
sections.translation: What will you do ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.705c
sections.original: Dies mihi hic ut satis sit vereor
sections.translation: I ’ m afraid that this day won ’ t be long enough for me to execute it , so don ’ t suppose that I ’ ve now got leisure for relating it ; do you betake yourself off at once , for you are a hinderance to me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.708
sections.translation: I ’ ll go and see her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.708b
sections.original: Quid tu ? quo hinc te agis ?
sections.translation: What are you going to do ? Whither are you going from here ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.708c
sections.translation: Do you wish me to tell you the truth ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.708d
sections.translation: No , not at all ; ( aside .) he ’ s making the beginning of a long story for me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.709b
sections.translation: What will become of me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.710
sections.original: Eho tu impudens non satis habes quod tibi dieculam addo
sections.translation: Come now , you unreasonable person , are you not satisfied that I give you a little respite , by putting off his marriage ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.711b
sections.translation: But yet , Davus —

sections.label: Section 4.2.711c
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.712
sections.translation: That I may marry her —

sections.label: Section 4.2.712b
sections.translation: Absurd .

sections.label: Section 4.2.712c
sections.original: Huc face ad me ut venias si quid poteris .
sections.translation: Be sure to come hither ( pointing in the direction of his house .) to my house , if you can effect any thing .

sections.label: Section 4.2.713
sections.translation: Why should I come ? I can do nothing for you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.713b
sections.translation: But still , if any thing —

sections.label: Section 4.2.713c
sections.translation: Well , well , I ’ ll come .

sections.label: Section 4.2.713d
sections.translation: If you can ; I shall be at home .

sections.label: Section 4.2.714b
sections.original: Tu Mysis dum exeo parumper opperire hic .
sections.translation: Do you , Mysis , remain here a little while , until I come out .

sections.label: Section 4.2.715
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.715b
sections.translation: There ’ s a necessity for so doing .

sections.label: Section 4.2.715c
sections.translation: Make haste .

sections.label: Section 4.2.715d
sections.translation: I ’ ll be here this moment , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.716
sections.original: Nihilne esse proprium cuiquam ? Di vestram fidem :
sections.translation: That nothing can be secure to any one ! Ye Gods , by our trust in you ! I used to make sure that this Pamphilus was a supreme blessing for my mistress ; a friend , a protector , a husband secured under every circumstance ; yet what anguish is she , poor thing , now suffering through him ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.720
sections.original: Dolorem ! facile hic plus mali est , quam illic boni .
sections.translation: Clearly there ’ s more trouble for her now than there was happiness formerly . But Davus is coming out . ( Enter DAVUS from the house of GLYCERIUM with the child .) My good sir , prithee , what is that ? Whither are you carrying the child ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.722b
sections.translation: Mysis , I now stand in need of your cunning being brought into play in this matter , and of your address .

sections.label: Section 4.3.724
sections.translation: Why , what are you going to do ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.724b
sections.translation: Take it from me directly , and lay it down before our door .

sections.label: Section 4.3.725b
sections.translation: Prithee , on the ground ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.726b
sections.original: Ex ara hinc sume verbenas tibi
sections.translation: Take some sacred herbs [*] from the altar here , [*] and strew them under it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.727b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you do it yourself ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.728
sections.original: Quia si forte opus sit ad herum iurandum mihi
sections.translation: That if perchance I should have to swear to my master that I did not place it there , I may be enabled to do so with a clear conscience .

sections.label: Section 4.3.729b
sections.translation: I understand ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.730
sections.original: Nova nunc religio in te istaec incessit cedo ?
sections.translation: have these new scruples only just now occurred to you , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.731
sections.original: Move ocius te , ut quid agam porro intelligas .
sections.translation: Bestir yourself quickly , that you may learn what I ’ m going to do next . ( MYSIS lays the child at SIMO ’ s door .) Oh Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 4.3.732b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.732c
sections.translation: The father of the intended bride is coming in the middle of it all . The plan which I had first purposed I now give up . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.734
sections.translation: I don ’ t understand what you are talking about .

sections.label: Section 4.3.734b
sections.translation: I ’ ll pretend too that I ’ ve come in this direction from the right . Do you take care to help out

sections.label: Section 4.3.736
sections.original: Orationi ut quomque opus sit verbis vide .
sections.translation: the conversation by your words , whenever there ’ s necessity . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.737
sections.original: Ego quid agas nihil intelligo : sed si quid est
sections.translation: I don ’ t at all comprehend what you are about ; but if there ’ s any thing in which you have need of my assistance , as you understand the best , I ’ ll stay , that I mayn ’ t in any way impede your success . ( DAVUS retires out of sight .)

sections.label: Section 4.4.740
sections.original: Revertor postquam quae opus fuere ad nuptias
sections.translation: After having provided the things necessary for my daughter ’ s nuptials , I ’ m returning , that I may request her to be sent for . ( Seeing the child .) But what ’ s this ? I ’ faith , it ’ s a child . ( Addressing MYSIS .) Woman , have you laid that here ( pointing to the child .) ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.742b
sections.translation: Where is he ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.743
sections.translation: Don ’ t you answer me ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.743b
sections.translation: He isn ’ t any where to be seen . Woe to wretched me ! the fellow has left me and is off .

sections.label: Section 4.4.744b
sections.translation: Ye Gods , by our trust in you !

sections.label: Section 4.4.745
sections.original: Quid turbae est apud forum ? quid illic hominum litigant ?
sections.translation: what a crowd there is in the Forum ! What a lot of people are squabbling there ! ( Aloud .) Then provisions are so dear . ( Aside .) What to say besides , I don ’ t know .
